pattern: Precomputing how far the strictly-increasing prefix and strictly-decreasing suffix extend reduces the valid-split test at each cut to two O(1) lookups.
hints: A cut after index i is valid only if everything up to i is strictly increasing and everything from i+1 on is strictly decreasing.
Find the longest strictly increasing run from the front and the longest strictly decreasing run from the back; a cut is valid when both runs reach the cut.

Let inc be the last index of the increasing prefix and dec the first index of the decreasing suffix. For each split point i where i < inc+1 covers the prefix and i+1 >= dec, use a running prefix sum to evaluate |left - right| and keep the minimum.

edge_inputs.description: two valid splits, smaller difference wins
one early split invalid, two valid ones tie
minimum length, invalid (right cannot be decreasing of length one? always valid here)
strictly increasing whole array means only the last cut works
strictly decreasing whole array means only the first cut works
equal adjacent values break both monotonic runs
increase then decrease with a wide valid window

stress.description: random arrays that frequently produce both valid and invalid splits
stress.note: small value range makes equal-neighbour traps common

stress.description: mountain-shaped arrays so many cuts are valid
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, rng.randrange(2, size + 1))
    peak = rng.randrange(1, n)
    left = sorted(rng.sample(range(1, 100001), peak))
    right = sorted(rng.sample(range(1, 100001), n - peak), reverse=True)
    return [left + right]
stress.note: guarantees a long increasing prefix and decreasing suffix
